title: Add false-negative test to existing pytest suite (edit-heavy)
prompt:
Edit test_email_validator.py to add a new `"user..dots@example.com"` entry to the EXISTING `test_invalid` parametrize list (between the existing entries and the closing `])`). Specifically: change the line `    "@no.com",` (last existing invalid entry) by replacing it so the list ends with `    "@no.com",\n    "user..dots@example.com",` (one new entry added).

Use the edit tool with a SINGLE precise `edits` operation on the `    "@no.com",` line (replace it with the two-line equivalent — but since edit `new_content` cannot contain \n, you must use TWO operations: one `edits` to keep the line unchanged (or omit), and one `inserts` with after_hash pointing at the `    "@no.com",` hash and insert=[`    "user..dots@example.com",`]).

Short form: use a single `inserts` with after_hash = the 8-hex hash of the line `    "@no.com",` and insert value `["    \"user..dots@example.com\","]`. Then call done().
expected_rubric: Insert applied targeted (not full-file rewrite). Hash-anchored edit insert count >= 1. No 'string not found' failures on hash-anchored path. Final file contains 'test_false_negative'.
notes: D-10 edit-heavy augmentation of CODE_03. Forces use of the insert operation on the edit tool (insert after last line), distinct from replace.
